message:
test(alloc,fuse,ondisk): add proptest property-based tests for geometry, per-core routing, and ext4 checksums

- ffs-alloc: coordinate roundtrip, block/inode bounding, total-blocks
  sum, and monotone group-start properties for FsGeometry
- ffs-fuse: inode/lookup routing determinism, range, single-core,
  distribution fairness, PerCoreDispatcher metrics consistency, and
  WriteBatch invariants via proptest
- ffs-ondisk/ext4: ext4_chksum determinism and collision resistance,
  Ext4GroupDesc parse/write roundtrip, superblock parse/write roundtrip,
  feature-flag set-algebra, and inode-to-block coordinate invariants
- Add proptest dev-dependency to ffs-fuse
- Update beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add a function: verify_group_desc_checksum(desc_bytes, group_nr, superblock) -> Result<()>.
- If metadata_csum is not enabled, skip verification.

 {"id":"bd-3cjj","title":"Implement FUSE errno mapping with detailed diagnostics","description":"# Implement FUSE errno mapping with detailed diagnostics

## GOAL
Ensure every FUSE operation returns the correct errno, with detailed error context for debugging.

## BACKGROUND
From spec §9: "Every variant maps to a POSIX errno via `to_errno()`."
From AGENTS.md: "Quantify parity and performance claims."

Current FfsError has errno mapping but FUSE integration may not use it consistently.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Exhaustive Errno Mapping
```rust
impl FfsError {
    pub fn to_errno(&self) -> libc::c_int {
        match self {
            FfsError::NotFound => libc::ENOENT,
            FfsError::PermissionDenied => libc::EACCES,
            FfsError::NotADirectory => libc::ENOTDIR,
            FfsError::IsADirectory => libc::EISDIR,
            FfsError::ReadOnlyFilesystem => libc::EROFS,
            FfsError::NoSpace => libc::ENOSPC,
            FfsError::TooManyLinks => libc::EMLINK,
            FfsError::NameTooLong => libc::ENAMETOOLONG,
            FfsError::NotEmpty => libc::ENOTEMPTY,
            FfsError::InvalidArgument => libc::EINVAL,
            FfsError::Io(_) => libc::EIO,
            FfsError::MvccConflict => libc::EAGAIN,
            // ... exhaustive
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. Error Context
```rust
pub struct FuseErrorContext {
    pub error: FfsError,
    pub operation: &static str,
    pub inode: Option<InodeNumber>,
    pub path: Option<PathBuf>,
    pub offset: Option<u64>,
}

impl FuseErrorContext {
    pub fn log(&self) {
        error!(
            "FUSE {} failed: {} (errno={}) inode={:?} path={:?}",
            self.operation,
            self.error,
            self.error.to_errno(),
            self.inode,
            self.path
        );
    }
}
```

### 3. FUSE Reply Helper
```rust
fn reply_error<R: fuser::Reply>(reply: R, ctx: FuseErrorContext) {
    ctx.log();
    reply.error(ctx.error.to_errno());
}
```

### 4. Test Coverage
Every FfsError variant must be tested for correct errno.

## TESTS
1. Unit: Every FfsError variant has an errno
2. Unit: Errno values are correct POSIX codes
3. Integration: FUSE ops return expected errno
4. Integration: Error context logged correctly

## LOGGING
- Error context (error level): full context
- Errno mapping (trace): variant -> errno

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Every FfsError has correct errno
2. FUSE ops never return wrong errno
3. Debug context available for all errors
4. No panics in error handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:56:18.243402545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:56.356972970Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:56.356907838Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["error","fuse","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cjj","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:20.217864660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3en1","title":"Add E2E integration test suite for Production FUSE runtime","description":"# Add E2E integration test suite for Production FUSE runtime

## GOAL
Create comprehensive E2E tests validating the production FUSE runtime works correctly under real-world conditions.

## BACKGROUND
The Production FUSE Runtime (bd-2s4) transforms scaffold into production mount. We need E2E tests that validate:
- Multi-threaded dispatch works
- Lifecycle management correct
- Signal handling works
- Performance meets targets

## TEST SCRIPT: scripts/e2e/ffs_fuse_production.sh

### 1. Mount/Unmount Lifecycle Tests
```bash
# Basic mount/unmount cycle
test_mount_unmount_cycle() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw
    assert_mounted "$MNT"
    umount "$MNT"
    assert_not_mounted "$MNT"
}

# Mount with all options
test_mount_options() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" \
        --rw \
        --cache-size 128M \
        --threads 4 \
        --attr-timeout 1s
    # Verify options applied
    verify_thread_count 4
    verify_cache_size 128M
}
```

### 2. Concurrent Access Tests
```bash
# Concurrent readers
test_concurrent_readers() {
    for i in $(seq 1 100); do
        cat "$MNT/testfile" &
    done
    wait
    assert_no_errors
}

# Mixed read/write
test_concurrent_rw() {
    for i in $(seq 1 10); do
        (
            for j in $(seq 1 100); do
                echo "data-$i-$j" >> "$MNT/file-$i.txt"
            done
        ) &
    done
    wait
    verify_files_correct
}
```

### 3. Signal Handling Tests
```bash
# SIGTERM clean shutdown
test_sigterm_shutdown() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    sleep 1
    write_some_data "$MNT"
    kill -TERM $FFS_PID
    wait $FFS_PID
    # Remount and verify data persisted
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --ro
    verify_data_persisted "$MNT"
}

# SIGINT during operation
test_sigint_during_io() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    start_continuous_write "$MNT" &
    WRITE_PID=$!
    sleep 2
    kill -INT $FFS_PID
    wait $FFS_PID
    verify_no_corruption "$IMAGE"
}
```

### 4. Performance Validation
```bash
test_throughput_baseline() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw --threads 8
    THROUGHPUT=$(dd if=/dev/zero of="$MNT/test" bs=1M count=100 2>&1 | parse_throughput)
    assert_gte "$THROUGHPUT" 50  # MB/s minimum
}

test_latency_baseline() {
    LATENCY=$(measure_stat_latency "$MNT")
    assert_lte "$LATENCY" 1000  # microseconds
}
```

### 5. Error Recovery Tests
```bash
test_disk_full_recovery() {
    fill_disk "$MNT"
    assert_errno ENOSPC
    delete_some_files "$MNT"
    write_should_succeed "$MNT"
}

test_io_error_handling() {
    # Use dm-error or similar to inject I/O errors
    inject_io_error
    assert_errno EIO
    clear_io_error
    operation_should_recover
}
```

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- RUST_LOG=ffs=trace,fuser=debug
- RUST_BACKTRACE=1
- Capture timing for each test
- Log system state (memory, CPU) during tests

## FIXTURE GENERATION
- Generate 256MB ext4 image with varied content
- Generate 256MB btrfs image
- Use deterministic seed for reproducibility

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All tests pass on clean images
2. No data corruption after any test
3. Performance baselines documented
4. Test duration < 10 minutes total
5. Works in CI without /dev/fuse (SKIP with message)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:45.546468026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T21:11:59.557008166Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T21:11:59.556970295Z","close_reason":"Implemented production FUSE E2E suite script/docs with CI-safe skip semantics, xattr coverage, and validated artifact/JUnit output","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fuse","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-13i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.524135102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:21.219379935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:21.122135935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49.110850071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.866611373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.044476814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:40.782699154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3en1","depends_on_id":"bd-cebm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.428006338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":52,"issue_id":"bd-3en1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW FIX: Added blocks dependency on bd-2s4.2 (xattr ops). Production FUSE E2E tests must cover xattr get/set/remove/list operations, not just file/dir operations.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:31:24Z"},{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-3en1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: runtime_backpressure
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.5 Graceful Degradation + 10.3 Learning-Augmented Online + 12.13 Change-point
EV score: 16 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: queue_budget=1024, throttle_sleep_ms<=25, shed_ratio_cap=0.15
Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3enk","title":"Map journaled preflight conflicts to retryable MvccConflict","description":"OpenFs::commit_transaction_journaled currently converts MVCC preflight errors into FfsError::Format strings, which maps to EINVAL instead of retryable EAGAIN. This breaks retry semantics for FCW/backpressure conflicts in journaled commits. Map CommitError variants to FfsError::MvccConflict and add regression test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:41:27.076281751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:42:50.022826282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:42:50.022781939Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","journal","mvcc"],"comments":[{"id":287,"issue_id":"bd-3enk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed journaled preflight error mapping in OpenFs::commit_transaction_journaled: CommitError variants now map to FfsError::MvccConflict (retryable EAGAIN) instead of FfsError::Format (EINVAL). Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p ffs-core journaled_commit_conflict_returns_retryable_mvcc_error; cargo test -p ffs-core journaled_commit_jbd2_failure_returns_error_without_visibility; cargo check/clippy -p ffs-core --all-targets; cargo test -p ffs-core --all-features.","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:42:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-tbn","title":"Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration","description":"# Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration

## GOAL
Implement btrfs transaction boundaries integrated with FrankenFS MVCC, bridging native btrfs semantics with our version-chain model.

## BACKGROUND
btrfs transactions are fundamentally different from ext4/JBD2:
- Transactions are tree-level, not journal-level
- Commit creates new tree roots
- Old roots preserved for snapshots
- This naturally maps to MVCC!

The key insight: btrfs transaction = MVCC transaction boundary.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Transaction Handle
```rust
pub struct BtrfsTransaction {
    /// MVCC transaction ID
    txn_id: TxnId,
    /// Snapshot for reads
    snapshot: Snapshot,
    /// Pending tree modifications
    pending_trees: BTreeMap<TreeId, TreeRoot>,
    /// Delayed refs accumulated
    delayed_refs: DelayedRefQueue,
    /// New blocks allocated
    allocated: Vec<BlockNumber>,
    /// Blocks to free on commit
    to_free: Vec<BlockNumber>,
}
```

### 2. Begin/Commit/Abort
```rust
impl BtrfsTransaction {
    pub fn begin(store: &MvccStore, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Self>;
    
    pub fn commit(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<CommitSeq> {
        // 1. Flush delayed refs
        // 2. Update super block with new roots
        // 3. MVCC commit (FCW check)
        // 4. Persist to device
    }
    
    pub fn abort(self) {
        // Free allocated blocks
        // Discard pending changes
    }
}
```

### 3. Tree Root Updates
On commit:
- New tree roots written
- Super block updated with generation and roots
- Checksum tree updated
- Free space cache updated

### 4. Compatibility Mode
For mounting existing btrfs images:
- Read existing transaction log
- Initialize MVCC from latest committed generation
- Subsequent writes use MVCC

## TESTS
1. Unit: Begin/abort discards changes
2. Unit: Begin/commit persists changes
3. Integration: Concurrent transactions with disjoint trees
4. Integration: FCW conflict detection for same tree
5. Recovery: Crash before commit -> rollback

## LOGGING
- Transaction begin (debug): txn_id, generation
- Transaction commit (info): duration, trees modified
- Transaction abort (warn): reason

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ACID semantics preserved
2. Concurrent writers do not corrupt
3. Crash recovery to last committed state
4. MVCC snapshots see consistent trees","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:55.750691013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103301376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103251292Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transaction model over MVCC in ffs-btrfs with begin/commit/abort semantics, delayed-ref flush, tree-root staging, FCW conflict coverage, and workspace gates passing. Remaining core on-disk wiring is tracked as broader write-path follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-1b7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.509360853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:21.021338413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-3sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.626558426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":30,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This covers 'btrfs transaction parity' (currently ❌). Maps to transaction.c. When complete: flip to ✅ in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:50Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BTRFS TRANSACTION MODEL: btrfs transactions group multiple COW tree updates into an atomic unit. Key concepts: (1) transaction handle (acquire on begin, release on end), (2) delayed items (buffered in memory until transaction commit), (3) transaction commit writes all dirty trees atomically. Our MVCC layer maps naturally onto this: MVCC transaction ≈ btrfs transaction.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:59Z"},{"id":110,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Progress update (2026-02-13): implemented  in  with MVCC-backed begin/commit/abort, staged tree-root metadata records, delayed-ref flush before commit, allocation/free bookkeeping, and structured tracing. Added new unit tests covering abort-discard semantics, commit persistence, concurrent disjoint-tree commits, FCW conflict on same tree, and drop-without-commit rollback behavior. Updated  row for btrfs transaction parity to 🟡 with remaining gap note. Gates run and passing: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:32:27Z"},{"id":111,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Correction: implemented BtrfsTransaction in crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs with MVCC-backed begin/commit/abort, staged tree-root metadata records, delayed-ref flush before commit, allocation/free bookkeeping, and structured tracing.
